data.title: KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) beats Q3 earnings estimates, posting 41 cents/share vs expected 38 cents/share, with sales at $1.895 billion vs $1.881 billion. CEO Chris Gorman cites strong momentum, 17% revenue growth y-o-y, and 1,000 basis points of operating leverage.
data.description: KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) reported impressive third-quarter earnings, surpassing market projections. The company's adjusted earnings of 41 cents per share exceeded expectations of 38 cents per share. Quarterly sales reached $1.895 billion, surpassing estimates of $1.881 billion. KeyCorp's CEO, Chris Gorman, expressed satisfaction with the results, highlighting a 17% increase in adjusted revenue compared to the previous year. The company also achieved over 1,000 basis points of operating leverage in the quarter. This growth was primarily attributed to net interest income tailwinds and an increase in adjusted noninterest income. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Judge John D. Bates received the 41st Annual Devitt Award, highest judicial honor, at the Supreme Court.
data.description: Last night, the 41st Annual Devitt Award by the Dwight D. Opperman Foundation took place at the Supreme Court of the United States in Phoenix. Chief Justice John G. Roberts, Jr. presided over the ceremony which celebrated the Senior United States District Judge of the U.S. District.

data.title: BNY Mellon beats Q3 estimates with $1.91 adjusted EPS, $5.081B total revenue. CEO pleased with 9% YoY revenue rise.
data.description: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation, listed under NYSE as BK, announced its third-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations. In 2025, the company reported a diluted EPS of $1.88 and an adjusted diluted EPS of $1.91, outperforming the estimated $1.77. Total revenue increased by 9% year over year to $5.081 billion, exceeding the estimated $4.974 billion, driven by a 7% rise in fee revenue and an 18% growth in net interest income. The GAAP pre-tax operating margin stood at 36%, the return on equity at 13.7%, and the return on tangible common equity at 25.6%. CEO Robin Vince expressed satisfaction with the results, noting that BNY achieved record revenue of $5.1 billion, reflecting a 9% year-over-year increase supported by robust growth across various platforms.

data.title: ManpowerGroup beats Q3 earnings estimates with 83 cents/share, $4.634 billion in sales. Q4 estimates at 78-88 cents/share. CEO on revenue growth after 11 quarters of decline.
data.description: ManpowerGroup (MAN) exceeded expectations in their third-quarter earnings report, announcing earnings of 83 cents per share, surpassing the estimated 81 cents per share. The company's quarterly sales reached $4.634 billion, exceeding the predicted $4.600 billion. Looking ahead, ManpowerGroup projects fourth-quarter GAAP earnings to fall between 78 cents and 88 cents per share, above the market forecast of 78 cents per share. CEO Jonas Prising noted a significant development, stating, "After 11 quarters of decline, we returned to revenue growth in the third quarter due to stabilized demand in North America and Europe." For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: nVent Electric plc (NYSE: NVT) to release Q3 2025 financial results on Oct 31. Results on website at http://investors.nvent.com.
data.description: London-based nVent Electric plc (NYSE: NVT), a worldwide frontrunner in electrical connection and protection solutions, is set to announce its third quarter 2025 financial results on Friday, October 31, 2025. The comprehensive financial report will be accessible on the company’s official website at http://investors.nvent.com. An official news release will be circulated once the earnings materials are live, containing a direct link to these essential documents. Additionally, a conference call with industry analysts and investors is also scheduled.

data.title: Massive Bio urges Congress to classify ClinicalTrials.gov as essential amid shutdown, impacting millions of patients.
data.description: In Boca Raton, Florida, amid the current government shutdown, ClinicalTrials.gov, the primary database for medical research and clinical trials in the US, has been taken offline. This disruption has impacted millions of individuals with cancer and severe illnesses who rely on the platform to find critical clinical trials. Dr. Arturo Loaiza-Bonilla, Co-Founder and Chief Medical & AI Officer of Massive Bio, expressed concern over this development, emphasizing the importance of access to potentially life-saving trials. Massive Bio, a company acknowledged by the White House Cancer Moonshot initiative, has highlighted the urgency of reinstating access to valuable medical resources for patients.

data.title: Bladex (NYSE: BLX) invites participation in 3Q25 conference call on Oct. 29, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time.
data.description: An invitation from Banco Latinoamericano de Comercio Exterior, S.A. (NYSE: BLX) for a conference call to review their 3rd quarter performance has been extended. The event is scheduled for Wednesday, October 29, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time. This call will provide insights into the company's recent financial results and future outlook.
